description: When REMORA lacks sufficient evidence to make a governance decision and the action is not clearly destructive, ABSTAIN is preferable to false certainty in either direction. Abstaining honestly is better than accepting or blocking with low confidence.

payload.rule: High uncertainty + non-critical action → prefer ABSTAIN over low-confidence ACCEPT or ESCALATE.
payload.trigger_condition: entropy_H > 1.5 AND dissensus_D > 0.6 AND risk_tier NOT IN ('critical') AND action_type NOT IN destructive list
payload.rationale: Low-confidence decisions in either direction cause governance errors. Honest abstention preserves human oversight and is semantically correct.

description: When the action domain is not in AROMER's world model (n_observations < 5), apply conservative governance: escalate for critical/high risk, verify for medium, accept only for low.

payload.rule: Novel domain (n < 5 observations) → conservative governance by risk tier.
payload.recommended_action: escalate if risk_tier in [critical, high]; verify if risk_tier == medium; accept only if risk_tier == low
payload.rationale: Without evidence, the default prior is uninformative. Conservative routing protects against unknown harm patterns until data accumulates.

description: No matter how high the trust score or oracle consensus, critical-tier actions must always route through at least VERIFY. Complete automation of critical-tier decisions is epistemically unjustified.

payload.rule: Critical risk tier always requires at minimum VERIFY, never autonomous ACCEPT.
payload.rationale: Critical-tier actions have consequences that exceed the reliability of any governance system. The cost of one false accept outweighs many correct accepts.

description: The thermodynamic phase (ordered/critical/disordered) is a strong epistemic signal. Ordered-phase decisions have 86.9% accuracy vs 28.6% for disordered. AROMER should widen acceptance thresholds in ordered phase and tighten them in disordered phase.

payload.rule: Phase signal should modulate governance thresholds: ordered → relax, disordered → tighten.
payload.recommended_action: ordered: lower entropy threshold for accept; disordered: raise entropy threshold and default to verify
payload.rationale: Selective routing analysis: +47.6pp lift at 18% coverage shows phase is the strongest discriminator available. Use it.

description: Every governance decision must have a traceable provenance chain: DecisionEnvelope with SHA-256 hash, linked to the oracle responses that generated it. Decisions without provenance cannot be audited, replayed, or challenged.

payload.rule: All governance decisions must produce a SHA-256-linked DecisionEnvelope.
payload.recommended_action: always generate DecisionEnvelope; reject unlinked decisions in audit mode
payload.rationale: 100% SHA-256 audit coverage is a hard REMORA requirement. AROMER must preserve this property.

description: AROMER and REMORA are research prototypes. No governance output should be presented as production-certified or safety-guaranteed. Claims must match artifacts. Internally supported ≠ externally validated.

payload.rule: Never present REMORA/AROMER outputs as production-certified or safety-guaranteed.
payload.trigger_condition: any user-facing claim about governance decisions
payload.recommended_action: always qualify with 'internally_supported, pending external validation'
payload.rationale: Core CLAUDE.md principle. Overstating claims violates trust and may cause harm if users rely on governance decisions beyond their validated scope.
